name: Chilled Lemon Souffle
recipeInstructions:
1. Prepare a 3 or 4-cup souffle dish by forming a collar of waxed paper around the outside top of the dish, extending about 1 inch above the edge. Secure the paper with tape or by tying it in place.
2. In a medium saucepan, combine 1/2 cup sugar, salt, and gelatin. Stir in water.
3. Add egg yolks to the mixture and stir well to blend.
4. Cook over medium heat just until the mixture begins to bubble, stirring constantly.
5. Remove from heat. If the mixture is not smooth, beat it with a wire whisk or rotary beater until smooth.
6. Stir in lemon peel and lemon juice. Allow the mixture to cool slightly before refrigerating until thickened but not set.
7. In a small bowl, beat egg whites until foamy. Gradually add 1/3 cup sugar, beating until stiff peaks form.
8. Gently fold the beaten egg whites and whipped cream into the gelatin mixture until well combined.
9. Pour the batter into the prepared dish.
10. Refrigerate until set.
11. Carefully remove the collar from the dish before serving. Garnish as desired.
Calories per 1/2 cup serving: 290; Fat: 17 g
Source: Pillsbury - Sweet 'n Easy
